commit.message:
chore: replace `getConfigValue` with direct `getConfig()` property access (#3589)

`getConfigValue(path: string)` accepted arbitrary string paths and
returned `any`, making typos silently return `undefined` at runtime and
forcing callers to add unverified type annotations or casts.

## Changes

- **Deleted `getConfigValue`** from `get-config.ts` along with the
now-unused `lodash/get` import
- **Migrated all ~20 call sites** to direct `getConfig()` property
access, removing manual type annotations and casts that only existed to
compensate for the `any` return type:

```ts
// before
const docsLink = getConfigValue('monitor.docsLink');  // any; typo = silent undefined
const schemas = getConfigValue('pathAgnosticDecorations') as TPathAgnosticDecorationSchema[] | undefined;

// after
const docsLink = getConfig().monitor?.docsLink;        // string | undefined, compiler-checked
const schemas = getConfig().pathAgnosticDecorations;   // readonly TPathAgnosticDecorationSchema[] | undefined
```

- **Type fixes surfaced by the migration:**
- `link-patterns.ts`: replaced `.filter(Boolean)` with a type predicate
to properly narrow `ProcessedLinkPattern[]`
- `transform-trace-data.ts`: cast `readonly string[]` → `string[]` for
`orderTags` parameter
- `TopNav.tsx`: added `?? ''` fallback for `qualityMetrics.menuLabel`
(`string | undefined`)
- `DependencyGraph/index.tsx`: replaced `||` with `??` for
`dagMaxNumServices` fallback

- **Updated all test files** that mocked `getConfigValue` to mock the
`getConfig` default export instead, passing structured config objects
rather than scalar values

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>[chore]: `getConfigValue` provides no type safety for
config access</issue_title>
> <issue_description>## Problem
> 
> `getConfigValue` accepts an arbitrary string path and returns `any`:
> 
> ```typescript
> // get-config.ts
> export function getConfigValue(path: string) {
>   return _get(getConfig(), path);
> }
> ```
> 
> This means two classes of error go undetected at compile time:
> 
> **1. Mistyped or renamed paths are silently undefined at runtime**
> 
> ```typescript
> getConfigValue('monitor.menuEnable') // typo — returns undefined, no
error
> getConfigValue('storagecapabilities.metricsStorage') // wrong case —
returns undefined
> ```
> 
> A recent bug in this codebase was caused by exactly this:
`storageCapabilities.metricsStorage`
> was accessed through `getConfigValue` and the result was used as a
boolean gate, but the path
> was wrong in an earlier version of the code and silently returned
`undefined` (falsy), causing
> the Monitor page to always show the empty state.
> 
> **2. Return types are `any`, so callers must annotate manually or
cast**
> 
> ```typescript
> // Caller must add an explicit annotation just to get type safety:
> const config: MonitorEmptyStateConfig =
getConfigValue('monitor.emptyState');
> 
> // Or cast, which defeats the purpose entirely:
> const schemas = getConfigValue('pathAgnosticDecorations') as
TPathAgnosticDecorationSchema[] | undefined;
> ```
> 
> These manual annotations are not checked against the actual type at
that path in `Config` —
> they are just asserted. A refactor that changes the `Config` type will
not flag these call sites.
> 
> **Scope:** `getConfigValue` is called in ~20 places across the
codebase. `getConfig()` is used
> directly in a handful of places and is already safe by construction
(TypeScript checks property
> access against the `Config` type).
> 
> ## Proposal — Replace `getConfigValue` calls with direct `getConfig()`
access
> 
> For all call sites where the path is a fixed, known string, replace:
> 
> ```typescript
> // before
> const docsLink = getConfigValue('monitor.docsLink');
> const enabled: boolean =
getConfigValue('storageCapabilities.metricsStorage');
> ```
> 
> with direct property access:
> 
> ```typescript
> // after
> const docsLink = getConfig().monitor.docsLink; // type: string |
undefined
> const enabled = getConfig().storageCapabilities.metricsStorage; //
type: boolean
> ```
> 
> This requires no new infrastructure and is already how
`tracking/index.ts` and
> `TraceTimelineViewer/duck.ts` access config. The memoization in
`getConfig()` means there is
> no performance difference.
> 
> ## Suggested migration order
> 
> 1. Switch all `getConfigValue` call sites to direct `getConfig()`
property access, removing
> any manual type annotations or casts that were only needed to
compensate for the `any`
> return type. Affected files: `TopNav.tsx`,
`Monitor/ServicesView/index.tsx`,
> `Monitor/EmptyState/index.tsx`, `ThemeStorage.ts`,
`DependencyGraph/index.tsx`,
> `link-patterns.ts`, `path-agnostic-decorations.ts`,
`DeepDependencies/index.tsx`,
> `DeepDependencies/SidePanel/index.tsx`, `SearchForm.tsx`,
`SearchResults/AltViewOptions.tsx`,
> `TraceId.tsx`, `TraceDiffGraph.tsx`, `transform-trace-data.ts`,
`get-target.ts`,
>    `process-scripts.ts`.
> 2. Delete `getConfigValue` from `get-config.ts` and remove its export
from any index files.
> 3. Delete the `getConfigValue` tests, or convert them to test
`getConfig()` directly.
> </issue_description>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> </comments>
> 


</details>



<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->

- Fixes jaegertracing/jaeger-ui#3588

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->

customizing its development environment and configuring Model Context
Protocol (MCP) servers. Learn more [Copilot coding agent
tips](https://gh.io/copilot-coding-agent-tips) in the docs.

commit.message:
Fix tonearm not resetting on track loop

The tonearm read audioPosition from the Zustand store, which was only
set once at load time and never updated during playback. When a track
looped, the stale position stayed at the end, making the tonearm start
outside the record.

Now reads the LIVE position from DeckAudioPlayer.getPosition(), which
wraps correctly via modulo when the track loops. Also fixed the platter
rotation angle which had the same stale-position issue.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Kba draft review fixes (#20)

* kba-draft implementiert

* - test dateien entfernt
- struktur aufgeräumt
- README.md angepasst
- learning_mechanism.md plan erstellt
- desing fixes

* feat: add search questions generation with database migration and UI

Database & Backend:
- Add search_questions column migration in operations.py (ALTER TABLE for existing databases)
- Add /api/kba/drafts/{id}/replace endpoint in app.py
- Fix backward compatibility in kba_service.py (_table_to_draft, _draft_to_table)
- Add search questions generation to replace_draft workflow
- Fix NULL constraint errors by ensuring empty strings for required fields
- Update related_tickets validation: accept INC + 9-12 digits (was fixed at 12)

Frontend:
- Add Text component import to KBADrafterPage.jsx (fix TypeError)
- Add full-screen blur overlay with centered spinner during KBA generation
- Show overlay for both new draft creation and replacement operations
- Update styles: loadingOverlay with backdrop-filter blur effect

Documentation:
- Update kba_prompts.py: clarify related_tickets format with examples
- Update GENERAL.md: correct related_tickets format specification

Fixes #1 - KBA drafts not loading (missing DB column)
Fixes #2 - Replace endpoint not found (405 error)
Fixes #3 - Ticket ID validation too strict

* tickets in popup ansehen

* feat(kba-drafter): add ability to reset reviewed KBAs back to draft

- Add "Zurück zu Entwurf" button for reviewed status KBAs
- Add handleUnreview() handler to update status from "reviewed" to "draft"
- Import ArrowUndo24Regular icon for the unreview action
- Allow users to continue editing KBAs after review without deletion

This enables editing of reviewed KBAs that need changes before publishing.

* feat(kba-drafter): add ticket viewer, unreview, status filter, and UI improvements

- Add ticket viewer dialog to display original incident details
  * New "Ticket" button in KBA header with DocumentSearch icon
  * Modal dialog showing incident data (ID, summary, status, priority, assignee, notes, resolution)
  * Backend endpoint /api/csv-tickets/by-incident/<incident_id> for incident ID lookup
  * Frontend API function getCSVTicketByIncident()

- Add unreview functionality for reviewed KBAs
  * "Zurück zu Entwurf" button with ArrowUndo icon
  * Allows resetting reviewed KBAs back to draft status for further editing

- Redesign KBA overview list
  * Replace corner delete button with professional overflow menu (⋮)
  * Horizontal layout: content left, status badge right-aligned, menu button
  * Menu component with delete option

- Add status filter dropdown to KBA overview
  * Filter options: All, draft, reviewed, published
  * Dropdown in card header for easy filtering

- Align EditableList "Add" button width with input fields
  * Use invisible placeholder buttons for exact width matching
  * Ensures consistent layout regardless of allowReorder setting

Files modified:
- frontend/src/features/kba-drafter/KBADrafterPage.jsx
- frontend/src/features/kba-drafter/components/EditableList.jsx
- frontend/src/services/api.js
- backend/app.py

* fix(kba): fix draft deletion bug and add collapsible AutoGenSettings

- Fix delete draft error: use response.items instead of response.drafts
- Make AutoGenSettings card collapsible with chevron icon
  - Starts collapsed to reduce visual dominance
  - Smooth slide-down animation when expanded
  - Status badge visible in collapsed header
  - Clickable header with keyboard support (Enter key)

* fix(kba): auto-scroll to top when opening draft

When clicking on a draft from the list after scrolling down,
the page now automatically scrolls to the top with a smooth animation.
This ensures users always start at the beginning of the draft content.

* feat: replace browser confirms with custom modal dialogs for unsaved changes

Replace native window.confirm() with ConfirmDialog component for better UX
consistency and modern appearance. Adds centered warning modal when user
attempts to discard unsaved changes (close draft, switch to preview, or
load different draft).

Changes:
- Add unsavedChangesDialogOpen and pendingAction states
- Update toggleEditMode, loadDraft, and handleClose to trigger modal
- Add handleDiscardChanges and handleCancelDiscard handlers
- Add ConfirmDialog with warning intent at end of component

* fix: address code review issues and add KBA drafter e2e tests

Fixes:
- Fix CSV folder case mismatch (CSV -> csv) in app.py and operations.py
- Remove duplicate get_ticket_by_incident_id method in csv_data.py
- Replace inefficient len(session.exec().all()) with SQL COUNT(*) in kba_service.py
- Replace hardcoded placeholder credentials with env var lookups in kba_service.py
- Fix scheduler swallowing exceptions (remove bare raise, return None)
- Add settings reload at start of each scheduler run to fix race condition
- Add generation_warnings field to surface search questions failures to users
- Add schema migration for generation_warnings column

Tests:
- Add 19 Playwright e2e tests for KBA Drafter feature covering:
  page load, navigation, LLM health status, draft generation,
  draft display, draft list, editing, review workflow,
  duplicate handling, and backend API integration

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add LiteLLM fallback, Playwright tests, and remove OpenAI hard dependency

- LiteLLM is now the default LLM backend (no .env or API key needed)
- Multistage model fallback chain: claude-sonnet-4 → gpt-4o → gpt-4o-mini
- OpenAI SDK still used when OPENAI_API_KEY is explicitly set
- agents.py and workbench service use ChatLiteLLM when no OpenAI key
- Added csv_ticket_stats and csv_sla_breach_tickets to agent tools
- Added KBA Drafter to Playwright nav tests and menu screenshots
- Added e2e tests: publish, delete, status filter, ticket viewer
- 32 unit tests + 5 live integration tests for LLM service
- Updated .env.example with LiteLLM-first documentation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: SubSonic731 <alessandro.roschi@bit.admin.ch>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

The 'members: read' permission key added in #586 is not a valid
GitHub Actions permission, causing the workflow YAML to fail
validation. This broke auto-closing of PRs (#587, #588).

The repos.checkCollaborator API works with the default GITHUB_TOKEN
repo access — no extra permission is needed.

commit.message:
Skip INTL0003 PascalCase validation for test methods (#374)

INTL0003 was flagging test methods with underscores (e.g.,
`Method_Scenario_ExpectedResult`) as PascalCase violations. Test methods
commonly use underscores for readability and should be exempt.

**Changes:**
- Added `IsTestMethod()` check in `NamingMethodPascal` analyzer to skip
methods decorated with test attributes
- Uses namespace-based detection to automatically recognize test
attributes from xUnit, NUnit, MSTest, and TUnit frameworks
- Any attribute from the `Xunit`, `NUnit.Framework`,
`Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting`, or `TUnit.Core`
namespaces is automatically recognized
- Includes fallback to common test attribute name checking for test
environments where namespace metadata may be incomplete
- Added test coverage for MSTest, xUnit, and NUnit frameworks
- Updated docs to document the exception

**Example:**
```csharp
[TestClass]
public class FooTests
{
    [TestMethod]
    public void FooThing_IsFooThing_HasFooThing() // No longer triggers INTL0003
    {
        Assert.IsTrue(true);
    }
    
    public void myNonTestMethod() // Still triggers INTL0003
    {
    }
}
```

This namespace-based approach provides better maintainability and
automatically supports new test attributes added to these frameworks
without requiring code changes to the analyzer.

when I don't think it should</issue_description>

Skip instance internals during file hygiene review

Treat <instance> nodes as leaf nodes when walking the layer tree
for hygiene checks. Only inspect an instance's own name and
visibility — skip its children (hidden sub-layers, nesting depth,
child names) since those are owned by the component source.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Replace roller with baren burnishing animation

Authentic mokuhanga print process:
- Replace vertical roller bar with circular baren tool (bamboo-sheath
  radial gradient with conic coil pattern, shadow underneath)
- Baren sweeps in organic arcs across paper with circular sub-motions
  (sinusoidal vertical oscillation + small burnishing circles)
- Each pass has slightly different sweep pattern for variety

Sound design (3 distinct moments):
- Paper lay: soft thud (60Hz sine) + highpass paper rustle at Step 1
- Baren burnishing: 1.5s sustained bandpass friction noise with
  rhythmic amplitude pulsing matching circular motion
- Paper peel: existing noise sweep now triggers at Step 3 START
  (was incorrectly at animation END)

Print engine tuning for baren passes:
- Reduce multi-pass offset from 1.2px to 0.3px (eliminates blur)
- Adjust opacity: single=1.0, first pass=0.92, additional=0.35
  (adds ink richness/depth without visible doubling)

UI: Rename 'Impressions' to 'Baren Passes' in Printing Workbench
Status messages updated: 'Burnishing with the baren...'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix isort import ordering for hoisted module-level imports (#101)

* Initial plan

* fix: resolve CodeQL import alerts and update README.md for production accuracy

- Alert #130 (code_guardian_secure.py:81): Replace try/except import of
  AmaCryptographyMonitor with TYPE_CHECKING guard — the name is only
  used in string-form type annotations, so a runtime import is unnecessary.
- Alerts #124–#129 (test_ama_cryptography_monitor.py): Hoist
  `import ama_cryptography_monitor` to module level and remove six
  redundant local imports inside test methods.
- Alerts #120–#121 (test_code_quality_additions.py): Hoist
  `import ama_cryptography.secure_memory` to module level and remove two
  redundant local imports inside test methods.
- README.md: Update test file count 28→29, add CodeQL to CI security
  scanning entry, correct Last updated date to 2026-03-10.

Six defense layers with real-time 3R anomaly monitoring protect people, data, and networks against classical and quantum threats.

- Integrate skills grid into resume.astro between preview and form
- Add ResumeSkillsGrid.test.ts validating data integration

Move duplicated category key mapping to src/utils/skills.ts
for single source of truth between resume.astro and tests.

Feature/export image (#14)

* feat: add image export (PNG & SVG) with font rendering support

- Add SVG/PNG export using @excalidraw/utils and @resvg/resvg-js
- Implement DOM polyfills (jsdom, FontFace, Path2D, document.fonts)
- Fix font rendering: intercept fetch() for local font loading,
  set EXCALIDRAW_ASSET_PATH, add unicodeRange to FontFace polyfill,
  pass fontDirs to resvg-js for proper text rendering in PNG
- Add 'export' CLI command and --export-as flag on 'create' command
- Support options: background, dark mode, padding, scale, embed scene
- Add programmatic API: exportToSVG, exportToPNG, exportImage
- Add 153 tests (unit + integration) covering all export functionality
- Update README with export documentation and examples

* Update src/exporter/dom-polyfill.ts

Co-authored-by: greptile-apps[bot] <165735046+greptile-apps[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update tests/helpers/fixtures.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: rename 'export' to 'convert', remove '-F' shorthand, rename '--export-scale' to '--scale'

- Rename CLI command 'export' → 'convert'
- Remove '-F' shorthand from --format option (use --format only)
- Rename '--export-scale' → '--scale' on both create and convert commands
- Update README documentation to match new CLI interface
- Update all CLI integration tests

* refactor: rename --dark-mode to --dark, rename export functions to convert*

* chore: add docstrings, pin dependencies, fix engines field, fix console.error concurrency leak

* docs: update README with node requirements, fix stale references

* refactor: rename exportScale to scale, exportWithDarkMode to dark in ExportOptions

* refactor: remove --export-as from create, rename --export-padding to --padding

- Remove --export-as and all export-related options from the create command
  (create now only produces .excalidraw files; use convert for images)
- Rename --export-padding CLI flag to --padding in convert command
- Rename exportPadding to padding in ExportOptions interface
- Remove 8 create-with-export tests, update README accordingly
- 145 tests passing across 8 test files

* docs: justify @excalidraw/utils 0.1.3-test32 prerelease pin

Add DEPENDENCIES.md explaining why @excalidraw/utils is pinned to
0.1.3-test32 (a prerelease version that is also the npm 'latest' tag):

- 0.1.2 ships a completely different API with no exportToSvg, no bundled
  fonts, no TypeScript types, and no ESM entry points
- 0.1.3-test32 is the only published version that provides the
  exportToSvg() function, bundled TTF fonts, and type declarations that
  our convert pipeline depends on
- Pinned without caret to avoid untested prerelease drift

Also add a note in README Requirements section pointing to DEPENDENCIES.md.

* Update src/exporter/dom-polyfill.ts

Co-authored-by: greptile-apps[bot] <165735046+greptile-apps[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: code review fixes

- Fix CLI version to match package.json (1.1.0)
- Add 'image' to valid node types in JSON parser
- Deduplicate anchor offset logic (elk-layout → generator)
- Replace dynamic imports with static imports in index.ts
- Pass edge label ID through factory instead of post-hoc mutation
- Filter undefined values from style mappers
- Guard against zero scaledWidth in PNG export
- Remove unused naturalHeight variable
- Fix getMimeType to handle URL query params
- Update README requirements to match engines field

* fix: address all PR review comments from CodeRabbit, Copilot, and Greptile

- Fix package-lock.json caret inconsistency for @excalidraw/utils (Copilot)
- Add date stamp to DEPENDENCIES.md dist-tag claim to prevent staleness (CodeRabbit)
- Harden path traversal guard with backslash rejection in dom-polyfill (CodeRabbit)
- Rewrite swapExtension using node:path to fix bug with dotted directories (CodeRabbit)
- Replace import.meta.dirname with Node 18-compatible fileURLToPath (CodeRabbit)
- Add test coverage for swapExtension edge cases with dotted paths

Made-with: Cursor

* fix: make polyfill initialization concurrency-safe

Gate ensureDOMPolyfill with a shared init promise so concurrent callers
don't race and run setup multiple times. Addresses CodeRabbit review.

Made-with: Cursor

* Update src/index.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address new Copilot review comments

- Use reference counting for console.error suppression so concurrent
  async convertToSVG calls don't restore prematurely
- Bump engines to >=20.19.0 to match jsdom v28 Node.js requirement
- Update @types/jsdom from ^27 to ^28 to match runtime jsdom v28

Made-with: Cursor

* fix: scope image exporter polyfills safely

Make export runtime shims temporary so modern Node globals like navigator do not crash initialization and host globals are restored after each export. Document the Node 20.19 floor and keep @excalidraw/utils because SVG rendering and packaged fonts are still required.

Made-with: Cursor

repository.name: excalidraw-cli
repository.description: CLI for creating Excalidraw flowcharts programmatically

commit.message:
Mobile improvements: autocomplete dropdowns, PWA meta tags, responsive layout

- Add PWA meta tags (apple-mobile-web-app-capable, theme-color)
- Replace HS datalist with custom fuzzy autocomplete dropdown
- Replace Yeshiva select with custom autocomplete + hidden input
- Add mobile two-row header with scrollable nav tabs
- Add touch improvements (tap highlight, 16px inputs, safe-area)
- Add responsive bottom-sheet modal on mobile
- Expand HS_ALIASES with common school abbreviations
- Filter phone-like values from HS lists
- Add fuzzy search to overlay student list
- Restore compareSet outline on grid rebuild
- Add YESHIVA_NAME_FIXES for casing normalization
- Remove obsolete populateHsDatalist function

fix: pass undefined flags to computeCashCreditDeltas so detectFlags runs

The \ lags ?? {}\ fallback produced a truthy empty object, which
caused computeCashCreditDeltas to skip detectFlags entirely. All CC
transactions were classified as purchases — payments, refunds, and
interest/fees were never separated out. Summaries need a rebuild after
this fix (clear + build migration).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add autocomplete dropdowns, mobile improvements, and UX enhancements

- Add PWA meta tags (apple-mobile-web-app, theme-color)
- Add CSS for HS/Yeshiva custom autocomplete dropdowns with keyboard nav
- Add mobile navbar two-row layout (header-row1) with responsive styles
- Add FAB safe-area padding, touch improvements, stats chart scroll
- Replace HS datalist with custom autocomplete (fuzzy scoring, highlights)
- Replace yeshiva select with autocomplete input + hidden value field
- Expand HS_ALIASES with common school abbreviations
- Add YESHIVA_NAME_FIXES map for display name normalization
- Filter phone-number-like values from HS list (HS_PHONE_RE)
- Restore compareSet visual indicators on yeshiva grid rebuild
- Remove populateHsDatalist (replaced by initHsAutocomplete)
- Improve overlay search with fuzzy char-sequence matching
- Consolidate duplicate HS_PHONE_RE constant; rename scoreHsMatch/highlightHsMatch to generic scoreMatch/highlightMatch
